description: Cross-binding DAE binding-contract fixtures per discretization RFC §12 (gt-q7sh). Each fixture is an ESM document that, after discretize() is applied, contains algebraic equation(s) alongside differential equation(s). Bindings MUST either (a) accept the DAE and return a discretized document whose `metadata.system_class` is `"dae"`, or (b) abort with error code `E_NO_DAE_SUPPORT` when DAE support is disabled (env var `ESM_DAE_SUPPORT=0` or a binding-specific flag). Silent success with `system_class` absent or equal to `"ode"` is a test failure — that indicates the binding dropped the algebraic constraint.

fixtures.description: One differential equation (dx/dt = -k*x) plus one algebraic observed equation (y = x^2). dae_support=true: output metadata.system_class == "dae", dae_info.algebraic_equation_count == 1. dae_support=false: abort with E_NO_DAE_SUPPORT.
Baseline: the same scalar ODE with no algebraic constraint. Output metadata.system_class == "ode"; E_NO_DAE_SUPPORT is NOT emitted under dae_support=false.

note: RFC §12 requires Julia and Rust to implement both the DAE-success path (metadata.system_class == "dae") and the disabled-DAE path (E_NO_DAE_SUPPORT). Python, Go, and TypeScript MAY stub the success path (return E_NO_DAE_SUPPORT always) in v0.2.0 but MUST emit the exact error code on the failure path. Only Julia is gated as required here because discretize() lands binding-by-binding (RFC §13.1); other bindings pick up this fixture as they implement their own discretize().
